pick.rationale: UCLA holds superior advanced metrics with KenPom and Torvik rankings around 25-27 versus UCF's 54-58, reflecting better overall efficiency from a rugged Big Ten schedule. The Bruins' projected edge of 6+ points aligns with their status as a 5.5-point favorite, and key contributors Donovan Dent and Tyler Bilodeau are expected to be available after minor injuries. While UCF's Big 12 experience and guard play give them a credible upset path that has convinced some experts like Jay Bilas to pick them, the quality gap and neutral-site metrics favor UCLA advancing in this 7/10 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Team quality and matchup analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Metrics strongly favor UCLA but upset risk is acknowledged given historical 7/10 trends and expert picks.
evidence: UCLA KP rank 27 (adjEM 21.64), Torvik 24; UCF KP 54 (adjEM 15.05), Torvik 58
UCLA record 23-11, UCF 21-11; UCLA -5.5 betting favorite
Dent calf strain and Bilodeau knee strain both expected to play per reports
Jay Bilas picks UCF but most data points to UCLA edge

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

They will play UCLA in the first round. ESPN analyst Jay Bilas predicts UCF will defeat UCLA in the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. The Knights, a No. 10 seed, are matched against the No. 7 seed Bruins in Philadelphia. He does not expect UCF to advance past the second round, forecasting a loss to UConn.
